mnemonics.title: Remember Unary vs Binary Operations
mnemonics.content: Unary: SEPR (Selection, Extension/Projection, Pseudo, Rename) - just ONE relation. Binary: UICD (Union, Intersection, CartProd, Division) - needs TWO relations.

mnemonics.title: Selection Rememberer
mnemonics.content: Selection = Horizontal filter (like picking ROWS). Think of 'σ' as 'S' for 'Select rows' or 'Sigma' for 'sifting' through data.

mnemonics.title: Projection Rememberer
mnemonics.content: Projection = Vertical filter (like picking COLUMNS). Think of 'π' as 'P' for 'Pick columns' or 'pi' as 'pieces' of the table.

acronyms.usage: The mathematical query language for relational databases
Horizontal filtering operation - extracts rows satisfying condition
Vertical filtering operation - extracts specific columns
Renames relation or its attributes to avoid ambiguity
Combines tuples from two relations, removes duplicates
Returns common tuples from both relations
Creates all possible combinations of tuples from two relations
Combines related tuples from two relations based on condition
Returns tuples matching ALL tuples in second relation (for-all queries)
General join condition using any comparison operator (<, >, =, ≤, ≥, ≠)
Preserves all left tuples, matches right tuples where possible, NULL otherwise
Preserves all right tuples, matches left tuples where possible, NULL otherwise
Preserves all tuples from both relations, NULL where no match

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a table with a highlighter: Selection highlights entire rows (horizontal), Projection highlights entire columns (vertical). Draw a grid and shade rows for Selection, columns for Projection.
Think of a multiplication table. If you have colors (Red, Blue) and sizes (S, M, L), the product gives all 6 combinations (Red-S, Red-M, Red-L, Blue-S, Blue-M, Blue-L).
Think of matching puzzle pieces. Two tables with common attribute 'dept_id' join like two puzzle halves snapping together on matching dept_id values.
Division is like asking 'Which students have taken ALL courses?' - you need course list (S) and check each student against every course.
Draw Venn diagram: Left join keeps all of left circle + intersection; Right keeps all of right + intersection; Full keeps everything.

analogies.analogy: Like using a filter in a coffee maker - only liquid (rows) that pass the filter (condition) come through.
Like selecting specific columns in an Excel sheet to copy, ignoring all other columns.
Like creating all possible outfits from your shirts (3) and pants (4) - you get 12 combinations.
Like matching dates in a dating app - you pair people based on compatibility (join condition).
Like finding siblings with the same last name - automatically matches on the common attribute (surname).
Like finding students who passed ALL exams - for every exam, they must have a passing score.
Like a class photo where everyone is included even if they don't have a partner - unmatched people still appear (with empty space/NULL).
